front: Who is Ewing Lusk and what is his primary contribution to parallel computing?
back: Ewing Lusk is a renowned computer scientist known for his fundamental contributions to the development of the Message Passing Interface (MPI), the dominant standard for communication in parallel computing systems.

front: What is the name of the high-performance computing research group co-founded by Ewing Lusk at Argonne National Laboratory?
back: The Mathematics and Computer Science (MCS) Division at Argonne, where he co-led the team that created MPICH, a pioneering and widely used implementation of the MPI standard.

front: What is MPICH and why is it significant?
back: MPICH is a high-performance, portable implementation of the MPI standard. It is significant because it served as the foundation and reference model for many other MPI implementations used worldwide in supercomputing clusters.

front: Name a key publication co-authored by Ewing Lusk that is central to parallel computing education.
back: "Using MPI: Portable Parallel Programming with the Message-Passing Interface" (and its sequels, like "Using MPI-2" and "Using MPI-3"), which are essential textbooks for learning parallel programming with MPI.

front: Beyond MPI, what other parallel computing paradigm did Lusk contribute to?
back: He was also a key contributor to the development of the PGAS (Partitioned Global Address Space) language Unified Parallel C (UPC) and co-authored the book "Languages and Compilers for Parallel Computing".
